zh.lifeSummary: 當Agnès VAN BEVEREN於大約1190年出生在比利時奧斯吉福拉安德倫貝福倫-瓦阿斯時，她的父親Diederik II VAN BEVEREN39歲，母親Ade de Coucy34歲。她於大約1215年與Gérard III VAN GRIMBERGEN結婚。他們至少是1個女兒的父母。她去世於大約1230年在比利時福拉阿姆-布拉班特格利波爾根享年42歲。

nameMeanings.name: VAN
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Anh, Thanh, Hung, Minh, Vinh, Dung, Hue, Huong, Lan, Quang, Tan, Thuy, Hong, Chi, Hang, Ho, Pok, Sang, Bai, Chin, Ching, Chu, Chuan, Dong.
Vietnamese (Vạn): from the Chinese surname 萬, see Wan 1.
Vietnamese (Văn): from the Chinese surnames 文 or 聞, see Wen 2 and 3, and also 5 below.

variants.name: Wan
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Hong, Yi, Qi, Qiang, Wai, Yiu, Yuk, Chien-Min, Fang, Hin, Jichun, Li, Man, Chung, Min, Yiping, Pak, Sik, Sung Man.  Vietnamese Tin, Bang, Da, Hien, Hoi, Lan, Minh Quang, Sau, Tich, Tung, Yaping, Yuet.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 萬, which means ‘ten thousand’ literally or ‘very great number’ figuratively: (i) from the personal name of Bi Wan (畢萬), an official in the state of Jin (located in present-day Shanxi province). He was said to be a descendant of Bi Gong Gao (known as Duke of Bi), the 15th son of the virtuous King Wen of Zhou (reined 1152–1056  BC ). Bi Wan was later granted the fiefdom of Wei (魏), which in 403  BC  became the state of Wei (located in present-day Shanxi, Shaanxi, Henan and Hebei provinces). Some of his descendants adopted Wei (魏) as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his personal name, as their surname. (ii) from the personal name Wan (萬), style name of Rui Bo (芮伯, the Earl of Rui), an official of the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ), who was granted the fief Rui (located in Ruicheng in Shaanxi province). Some of his descendants inherited Rui (芮), the name of the fief, as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his style name, as their surname. (iii) from the placename Wan (萬), the name of a fief (located in Juxian in Shandong province) granted during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ) to an official in the ancient state of Chu. (iv) from the first element of the name of the Wan Si (萬俟) family, who lived in the downstream areas of the Yellow River during the Northern Qi dynasty (550–577  AD ). (v) from the second element of the name of the Tu Wan (吐萬) family, who are members of the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 完, meaning ‘complete’ in Chinese: (i) said to be borne by the descendants of Huan Zhui, a grandson of the Duke Huan of Song (ruler of the state of Song, died 651  BC ). (ii) from Wan (完), the name of Qu Wan (屈完), an official in the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn Period (770–476  BC ). (iii) shortened form of the compound surname Wan-Yan (完顔), a surname from minority ethnic groups such as Juchen ethnic group in ancient northern China.

nameMeanings.name: Agnès
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Latinized version of the Greek name Hagnē, from the feminine form of the adjective hagnos ‘pure, holy’. This was the name of a young Roman virgin martyred in the persecutions instigated by the Roman emperor Diocletian in ad 303 . She became a very popular saint in the Middle Ages. Her name was early associated with Latin agnus ‘lamb’, leading to the consistent dropping of the initial H- and to her representation in art accompanied by a lamb. The colloquial form Annes led to some confusion with Ann(e) in earlier centuries. Frequent in the medieval period, the name was revived in the 19th century, and has been especially popular in Scotland. See also Annis .

en.lifeSummary: When Agnès VAN BEVEREN was born about 1190, in Beveren-Waas, East Flanders, Belgium, her father, Diederik II VAN BEVEREN, was 39 and her mother, Ade de Coucy, was 34. She married Gérard III VAN GRIMBERGEN about 1215. They were the parents of at least 1 daughter. She died about 1230, in Grimbergen, Flemish Brabant, Belgium, at the age of 42.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Agnès VAN BEVEREN est née environ 1190, à Beveren-Waas, Flandre-Orientale, Belgique, son père, Diederik II VAN BEVEREN, avait 39 ans et sa mère, Ade de Coucy, avait 34 ans. Elle a épousé Gérard III VAN GRIMBERGEN environ 1215. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fille. Elle est décédée environ 1230, à Grimbergen, Brabant Flamand, Belgique, à l’âge de 42 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Agnès VAN BEVEREN um 1190 in Beveren-Waas, Ostflandern, Belgien geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Diederik II VAN BEVEREN 39 und ihre Mutter Ade de Coucy 34. Sie hat um 1215 Gérard III VAN GRIMBERGEN geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Tochter. Sie starb um 1230 im Alter von 42 Jahren in Grimbergen, Flämisch Brabant, Belgien.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Agnès VAN BEVEREN è nata il circa 1190 nel Beveren-Waas, Fiandra Orientale, Belgio, sua padre, Diederik II VAN BEVEREN, aveva 39 anni e sua madre, Ade de Coucy, aveva 34 anni. Lei sposato Gérard III VAN GRIMBERGEN circa 1215. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlia. Morì circa 1230, nel Grimbergen, Vlaams-Brabant, Belgio, all'età di 42 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Agnès VAN BEVERENが約1190年にベルギーオオスト-vラアンデレンベバーエン-ワアスで生まれた時，父Diederik II VAN BEVERENは39歳，母Ade de Coucyは34歳であった。約1215年にGérard III VAN GRIMBERGENと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の娘の親であった。彼女は約1230年にベルギーvラアムス-ブラバントグリンバージェンで42歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Agnès VAN BEVEREN(이)가 대략 1190년 벨기에 스트-블라아더 비버-와아스에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Diederik II VAN BEVEREN(은)는 39세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Ade de Coucy(은)는 34세였다. 그녀는 대략 1215년 Gérard III VAN GRIMBERGEN(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 대략 1230년 벨기에 블라아스-브라반트 그림버겐에서 42세의 나이로 사망했다.

mg.about 1190–about 1230: tany ho any amin'ny 1190–tany ho any amin'ny 1230

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Agnès VAN BEVEREN nasceu aproximadamente 1190, em Beveren-Waas, Flandres Oriental, Bélgica, seu pai, Diederik II VAN BEVEREN, tinha 39 anos e sua mãe, Ade de Coucy, tinha 34 anos. Ela casou-se com Gérard III VAN GRIMBERGEN aproximadamente 1215. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filha. Ela faleceu aproximadamente 1230, em Grimbergen, Vlaams-Brabant, Bélgica, com 42 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Agnès VAN BEVEREN родилась около 1190 г. в местности бивирен-ваас, уст-влаандирен, Бельгия, её отцу по имени Diederik II VAN BEVEREN было 39 лет, а матери, которую звали Ade de Coucy, 34. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Gérard III VAN GRIMBERGEN около 1215 г. У них была по крайней мере 1 дочь. Она умерла около 1230 г. в местности гримбиржен, влаамс-брабант, Бельгия в возрасте 42 лет.

sm.about 1190–about 1230: e tusa o le 1190–e tusa o le 1230

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Agnès VAN BEVEREN nació aproximadamente 1190, en Beveren-Waas, Flandes Oriental, Bélgica, su papá, Diederik II VAN BEVEREN, tenía 39 años y su mamá, Ade de Coucy, tenía 34 años. Se casó con Gérard III VAN GRIMBERGEN aproximadamente 1215. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hija. Murió aproximadamente 1230, en Grimbergen, Vlaams-Brabant, Bélgica, a la edad de 42 años.
